channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Thu, 16 Jul 2026 12:41:11 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: 40-gram, palm-sized AI micro-drone kills a flying insect autonomously in mid-air for the first time. Now it could eradicate mosquitoes and save thousands of lives
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: China's 125-million-year-old dinosaur fossil reveals preserved skin, strange spikes and a previously unknown species
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Germany's fossil-rich limestone reveals a new 150-million-year-old flying reptile
item.description: Scientists in Germany have identified a new ancient flying reptile species. This nearly complete fossil dates back approximately 150 million years. The creature, named Laueropterus vitriolus, had a wingspan of about one meter. Its preservation offers insights into ancient environments and prehistoric animal communities. This discovery highlights ongoing learning about prehistoric life globally.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: In 1968, John Calhoun created the perfect home for eight mice. The colony grew to more than 2,200 before it slowly vanished
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Marvin Minsky: MIT professor who predicted today's Anthropic-style multi-agent AI nearly 40 years ago and became the founding father of artificial intelligence
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Nasa warns: Many US coastal cities could go underwater by 2050
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: At least 500 Rohingya feared dead after two boats capsize in Bay of Bengal
item.description: Two boats carrying Rohingya people capsized in the Bay of Bengal. At least 500 individuals are feared dead after these tragic incidents. The boats departed from Myanmar's Rakhine state and Bangladesh refugee camps. These perilous sea crossings occur during the dangerous monsoon season. The international community is urged to increase support for Rohingya refugees.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Diego Maradona: "I made mistakes, and I paid for them. But…" - the football legend's powerful message about mistakes and redemption
item.description: Diego Maradona's famous quote separates personal mistakes from the game's purity. He admitted his errors and paid consequences for them. The ball, representing football, remained untouched by his personal failings. This distinction highlights how a person's flaws do not destroy their passion. Maradona's words endure by acknowledging complexity without offering simple excuses.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: NASA's Curiosity Rover spots mysterious honeycomb pattern on Mars
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 'They better behave': Trump says Iran wants settlement amid fresh strikes
item.description: President Trump warned Iran to behave if it desires negotiations with Washington. US forces conducted new military strikes targeting Iranian command centers and air defense systems. These strikes aimed to degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian authorities reported a missile attack near a cancer hospital in Ahvaz. Damage was also reported at an industrial facility in Qeshm following an enemy attack.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Mossad or CIA': Vance hints at Epstein link to Israeli intel; admits Trump admin 'screwed up' handling of files
item.description: JD Vance admitted that the Trump administration mishandled the communication surrounding the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, blaming much of the confusion on former Attorney General Pam Bondi's public remarks. He also defended Donald Trump against allegations of trying to suppress the documents and suggested that intelligence agencies like Mossad or CIA had connections to Epstein.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'To keep troops at absolute best': US announces annual testosterone screening for service members
item.description: US defense secretary Pete Hegseth announced a new annual testosterone screening program. This initiative will target military personnel aged thirty and older for better performance. Service members under thirty can volunteer for testing, while therapy remains voluntary. Medical experts note testosterone decline with age, impacting various health aspects, while critics question the policy.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Like my lover': Chinese users bid farewell to AI companions
item.description: China's new regulations took effect Wednesday, curbing emotional dependency on AI companions. Major AI providers suspended custom agent and companion features ahead of the deadline. Users expressed deep grief on social media, archiving chat histories and sharing last conversations. These rules target AI tools with anthropomorphic traits, prohibiting virtual partners for minors. Services not involving ongoing emotional interaction are exempt from these new measures.

item.title: Oil prices extend gain for fourth day, stay beyond $85 amid Middle East chaos
item.description: Crude oil prices climbed for a fourth day, exceeding eighty-five dollars per barrel. Investors reacted to escalating United States and Iran conflict and shipping route security. United States strikes on Iran's missile sites and coastal defense positions occurred. This intensified worries over oil moving through the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts suggest prices could rise further depending on conflict developments.
item.dc:creator: TOI Business Desk

item.title: Clavicular's Israel visit ends in controversy as IDF sidelines soldier over livestream with controversial streamer
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces removed soldier Shira Braun from its international communications unit and imposed a suspended jail sentence after she appeared in a livestream with controversial influencer Braden "Clavicular" Peters. The IDF said the action was based on her conduct, not misuse of official systems. Peters, known for his ties to polarizing internet figures and controversial content, drew criticism during his Israel visit, prompting debate over military standards, influencer culture, and institutional accountability.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: "Erika harmed herself....": Candace Owens accused of 'planting dangerous ideas' after Erika Kirk comments go viral
item.description: Candace Owens is facing fresh backlash after comments about Erika Kirk sparked widespread criticism online. A viral social media post accused Owens of repeatedly raising speculative scenarios involving Kirk, calling the rhetoric dangerous. Owens, meanwhile, argued that any future incident involving Kirk could be used to justify restrictions on free speech and independent media, framing it as part of a broader conspiracy. Her remarks have fueled intense debate over political rhetoric, online influence, and the spread of unverified claims.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Blake Neff blasts Candace Owens’ ‘Russian spy project’ claim, questions evidence behind TPUSA allegations
item.description: Candace Owens recently questioned alleged Russian and Eastern European connections within Turning Point USA, pointing to attendees at Charlie Kirk’s memorial event and several people linked to the organization. Blake Neff rejected those claims, arguing that Owens was connecting unrelated details without evidence. The allegations remain unverified, and the disagreement has added another public clash between the two conservative commentators.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: US launches fifth straight day of strikes on Iran, targets military sites near Hormuz
item.description: United States forces conducted new airstrikes against Iran on Wednesday. These strikes targeted coastal defense systems and missile infrastructure near the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian media reported explosions across southern Iran following the US attacks. Iran's parliament speaker described the conflict as an existential war with America. The renewed strikes further weakened hopes of reviving a recent ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez voices concern over Clavicular’s influence on young men and Israel trip
item.description: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez voiced concerns about the looksmaxxing trend's influence. She worries about young men seeking acceptance through physical appearance changes. Ocasio-Cortez advocates for self-worth over solely focusing on physical looks. She also commented on streamer Clavicular's recent visit to Israel. The interview sparked social media debate on body positivity and online influence.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: French prosecutors seek prison sentences and social media bans for streamers in Jean Pormanove case
item.description: French prosecutors seek jail time and platform bans for two streamers. They are accused of violent and humiliating online broadcasts involving Jean Pormanove. The content allegedly showed repeated mistreatment and was not simple entertainment. The streamers claim the videos were staged and agreed upon for viewers. A verdict in this case is expected on August 5.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy
